provenance.source_corpus: Plant Species Composition and Forage Production 14 Yr After Biosolids Application and Grazing Exclusion

source_spans.text: This paper examines the effects of a single surface application of biosolids (at 20 dry Mg ha /C0 1) on plant species composition, forage quality and quantity, and C stocks after 14 yr of rest in rangelands of the Central Interior of British Columbia.
More than two times the aboveground biomass of grasses and the percent cover of plant litter were found in the biosolids treatment relative to the control
An examination of biomass by functional group showed that the grass biomass was more than double in the biosolids plots
Bluebunch wheatgrass, the late-seral native grass species, had significantly increased cover and aboveground biomass in the biosolids treatment relative to the control
Bluebunch wheatgrass, the late-seral native grass species, had significantly increased cover and aboveground biomass in the biosolids treatment relative to the control
Kentucky bluegrass was significantly greater in the biosolids treatment in 2016, covering 26% of biosolids plots and < 1% of control plots
reductions in bare soil and microbiotic crust cover
reductions in bare soil and microbiotic crust cover
More than two times the aboveground biomass of grasses and the percent cover of plant litter were found in the biosolids treatment relative to the control
Significantly greater plant uptake of all macronutrients (C, N, P, K, S, Ca, and Mg); most micronutrients (B, Cu, Mn, Mo, and Zn); and Al occurred in the biosolids treatment.
P and Cu were the only two nutrients to be more concentrated in the biosolids-treated forage relative to the control forage
P and Cu were the only two nutrients to be more concentrated in the biosolids-treated forage relative to the control forage
N, Mg, and protein were more concentrated in the control forage
N, Mg, and protein were more concentrated in the control forage
aboveground plant biomass (i.e., vegetation) C stock was nearly double in the biosolids plots as compared with the control plots
The thatch on the biosolids plots represented a C stock of 6.4 Mg ha /C0 1... It was not present on the control plots.
The decrease in bare soil in both treatments in the first 3 yr of the experiment was likely a result of the exclusion of grazing
